_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised, autopilot local cron, 1930 batch)
_meta.note: VA LOCAL batch — 5 officials across 3 jurisdictions: (1) worth-remick VB Council D6 (Independent/Episcopal/Colliers commercial-RE; very sparse culture-war record but HARD TRUE PJ q0 affirmative pro-LE substation/officer-plus-up + HARD FALSE econ q2 voted YES on FY26 budget with 0.5% meal-tax hike he said he opposed); (2) steve-descano Fairfax Commonwealth's Attorney (D, RICH record — 8 cited FALSEs incl. PJ q0/q1/q2/q3 ('police unions = greatest institutional obstacle to real reform' + AEI 'America's Worst Prosecutor' + active DOJ civil rights probe May 2026), sanctity_of_life q1 ('I will not prosecute a woman for having an abortion' NYT/Twitter sanctuary declaration), border q2 (6-yr written office policy 'make charging and plea decisions that limit or avoid immigration consequences' — removed May 2026 before Capitol Hill testimony); +education CORRECTION: West Point/Temple Law/Army Aviation, not Naval Academy/Georgetown/Navy as some sources state); (3) belen-rodas Spotsy SB Chancellor (de facto D-aligned reform faction, MSW + Georgetown MPP + R-DMT social worker, Sunday-school teacher w/ husband — Christian denomination undisclosed; HARD FALSE FCS q2 voted with 5-0 reform majority on the 2024 library-content challenge-policy softening + named defendant in Peterson book-removal petition; HARD FALSE PJ q3 mental-health-crisis framing as campaign #2 priority + General Assembly lobbying); (4) carol-medawar Spotsy SB Courtland (de facto D-aligned reform, Energized for Change PAC + 7th CD Dems funded, replaced Twigg-bloc Abuismail, 28-yr public-school educator + VCU Ph.D. Instructional Leadership; HARD FALSE FCS q2 + q3 voted with reform majority on 2024 instructional-material challenge policy + book-policy loosening; HARD FALSE BM q2 + q4 explicit Democratic-PAC slate against the Youngkin-trans-policy majority — replaced loudest pro-policy vote Abuismail; HARD FALSE econ q2 voted FOR FY25 $480M budget 6-1 + Feb 2026 publicly defended central-office salaries vs supervisor cuts); (5) chris-yakabouski Spotsy BoS Battlefield (R verified — state GOP overruled local Spotsy GOP to give him 2025 nomination after he criticized them; 2025 Board Chair; 22-yr incumbent + small-biz home-remodeling; HARD TRUE SD q0 sitting Board member for Dec 10 2019 2A Sanctuary unanimous resolution + HARD TRUE SD q1 publicly proposed CCW fee cut 'strong believer in the Second Amendment'; HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q4 same 2A sanctuary vote refused to enforce Northam-trifecta gun-control state mandates; HARD FALSE econ q2 voted AGAINST FY25 73.43¢ rate that passed 4-3 + pushed for HIGHER 75.17¢ rate to fund schools more — fiscal-heterodox R catch). NET BATCH: 17 cited FALSE cells + 4 cited TRUE cells across 5 records. PARTY FIXES: rodas Unknown→D (de facto via Energized-for-Change PAC + 7th CD Dems + teachers' union endorsement + ran on the 2023 anti-Twigg reform slate); medawar Unknown→D (same slate, same Democratic PAC funding); worth-remick stays Unknown/Independent (de jure nonpartisan + no findable partisan endorsement); yakabouski Unknown→R (verified GOP nominee per Spotsy 2025 candidates list). PROFILE NOTE: Descano's brief said Naval Academy/Georgetown — actual public record is West Point BS + Temple JD + US Army Aviation Officer. Corrected here.

profile.religion: Episcopalian — leadership roles at Eastern Shore Chapel Episcopal Church (Virginia Beach), Diocese of Southern Virginia (TEC mainline; congregation publicly self-describes as 'Inclusive Church').
profile.education: Hampden-Sydney College, humanities; 4-year tennis athlete, 3x All-ODAC. Sorensen Institute for Political Leadership fellow at UVA.
profile.background: Born/raised Suffolk and Smithfield, VA; moved to Virginia Beach 1985. ~40-year commercial real estate career as Senior Vice President at Colliers International, specializing in industrial leasing/sales/development (29M+ sq ft leased/sold). Former VA State Senate legislative aide (1984). Head tennis coach at Frank W. Cox HS 2000–2006. Community boards: South Hampton Roads Habitat for Humanity, Green Run Collegiate Foundation, Virginia Beach Forum, Virginia African American Cultural Center. Married to Mary Gentry; two stepdaughters. First elected to Virginia Beach City Council District 6 Nov 2022 (43.2% in a 4-way race over Linwood Branch, Richard Kowalewitch, Cathy Porterfield); sworn Jan 1 2023; current term ends Dec 31 2026 — ON the Nov 2026 ballot. Council appointments: Advertising Advisory Committee, Hampton Roads Planning District Commission, Oceana Land Use Conformity Committee.

worth-remick.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Virginia Beach City Council District 6, sworn Jan 1 2023, term ends 12/31/2026 — ON Nov 2026 ballot. PARTY: stays Unknown / de jure nonpartisan; VPAP and elections database list all 2022 D6 candidates as Independent and no Republican/Democratic primary or endorsement evidence surfaced (no inference). RELIGION: Episcopalian at Eastern Shore Chapel Episcopal Church (TEC mainline, congregation self-describes as 'Inclusive Church' — congregational branding is biographical not a personal-position substitute). EDUCATION: Hampden-Sydney humanities + Sorensen Institute fellow. BACKGROUND: ~40-yr Colliers International commercial real estate broker (Sr VP, industrial focus), former VA State Senate legislative aide 1984, Cox HS head tennis coach 2000-06. HARD TRUE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared): publicly called for adding another police substation/precinct in the resort area, hiring more officers with dedicated funding, and stronger enforcement of existing laws including citations for public marijuana — affirmative LE backing beyond mere budget vote (yahoo.com/13newsnow May 2025 oceanfront curfew aftermath coverage). Quoted: 'we should never have a curfew' — disavowed extraordinary-policing tools in favor of more officers + substation buildout. HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted YES on the FY26 Virginia Beach budget (10-1 council vote) that included a 0.5% meals-tax increase, a revived boat registration tax, and a stormwater rate hike — even after publicly stating he did not support the meal-tax piece (whro.org 5/13/2025 + 13newsnow.com). Vote-against-restraint posture even with stated reservations — null'ing this on the grounds that he OPPOSED a piece would be too generous; the rubric prizes vote-bearing fiscal discipline. Parallel to the Norfolk-batch FALSEs on tax-rate-hold votes. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / election_integrity / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense — no quote-bearing positions located in available sources despite extensive social-media + meeting-minutes review. The Episcopal Eastern Shore Chapel 'Inclusive Church' branding is congregational not personal; biblical_marriage q4 stays null absent a Remick-signed Pride proclamation or trans-policy vote. He was NOT on council for the Jan 2020 VB 2A Sanctuary vote (predates him by 3 years). 1 TRUE / 1 FALSE on cited cells = 50% — coherent low-profile center-right/center-left independent Council member whose only firm public signals are affirmative pro-LE plus-up and FY26 tax-hike budget vote. Recommend revisiting after the Nov 2026 election cycle and any 2026-27 culture-issue council votes.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police record: publicly called for adding another police substation/precinct in the resort area, hiring more officers with dedicated funding, and stronger enforcement of existing laws including citations for public marijuana — affirmative LE backing beyond mere budget vote. Quoted disavowing future curfews 'we should never have a curfew' in favor of more police presence.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY26 Virginia Beach budget (10-1 council vote) that included a 0.5% meals-tax increase, a revived boat registration tax, and a stormwater rate hike — even after publicly stating he did not support the meal-tax piece. Voting FOR a tax-increase budget while saying you oppose part of it does not satisfy the budget-restraint cell (rubric-adverse on q2).

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no statement of faith located in his official county bio, campaign site, Wikipedia, or press coverage reviewed.
profile.education: B.S., United States Military Academy at West Point; J.D., Temple University Beasley School of Law. (NOTE: Some informal references state 'Naval Academy / Georgetown Law' — the public record per Wikipedia + consistent press coverage shows West Point + Temple Law.)
profile.background: Former U.S. Army Aviation Officer (NOT Navy as some informal sources state). Six-year federal prosecutor at the Department of Justice under the Obama administration, financial crimes. General counsel / COO at Paragon Autism Services. NAACP representative on the Fairfax County Police Civilian Review Panel. First elected Commonwealth's Attorney of Fairfax County, VA Nov 2019; sworn Jan 2020; re-elected Nov 7 2023 with 77% over Ed Nuttall (R); current term ends Dec 31 2027 — NEXT election Nov 2027. Married to Ryanne; daughter Charlotte. ~$600K of his 2019 primary cash came from George Soros's Justice & Public Safety PAC (~2/3 of total when combined with the New Virginia Majority PAC). Subject of a 2021-23 recall petition that did not reach the ballot. Subject of an active U.S. Department of Justice civil rights investigation opened May 7 2026 over a written office policy directing prosecutors to 'make charging and plea decisions that limit or avoid immigration consequences' (DOJ press release).

steve-descano.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Fairfax County Commonwealth's Attorney, sworn Jan 2020, re-elected Nov 7 2023 with 77% vs. Ed Nuttall (R), term ends 12/31/2027 — next election Nov 2027. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. EDUCATION CORRECTION: West Point BS + Temple Law JD (NOT Naval Academy/Georgetown as some informal references state — Wikipedia + WJLA + DCist + Fairfax Times all confirm West Point/Temple); served as US Army Aviation Officer (NOT Navy). The prototype 'Soros-funded progressive prosecutor' of the conservative rubric — ~$600K of 2019 primary cash from George Soros's Justice & Public Safety PAC plus New Virginia Majority PAC (~2/3 of total). HARD FALSE public_justice q0 (HIGH BAR cleared in REVERSE direction): tweeted 'There is no greater institutional obstacle to real reform than police unions. If you spend your career working for that obstacle, you don't believe in reform' (wjla.com) — affirmative anti-LE-institution advocacy attacking the police union directly. Clears the rubric's HIGH BAR for q0 in the rubric-adverse direction. HARD FALSE public_justice q1: 'From his first day in office, Steve has not asked for cash bail. After formally ending cash bail in 2020...' (stevedescano.com/results1); declines marijuana possession prosecution; 'I'm against the death penalty, period, full stop' (boltsmag.org); named 'America's Worst Prosecutor' by AEI; DOJ civil rights investigation opened May 7 2026 (justice.gov). Multi-vector decarceral/Soros-DA policy posture — cleanest local rubric-adverse hit on q1 in the entire VA batch. HARD FALSE public_justice q2: office policy avoids overcharging/mandatory minimums, refuses 3rd-strike enhancements for petty larceny, prioritizes diversion over conviction; multiple high-profile dismissals/reductions in violent cases (Richard Kenneth Cox, Marvin Morales-Ortez, Jalloh probation release) prompted recall petitions and the DOJ probe (fairfaxtimes.com + stevedescano.com/results1) — explicit failure-to-punish posture on the proportional-punishment cell. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: direct quote on addiction — 'addiction is an illness, not a crime' (boltsmag.org); created Diversion Coordinator and 'Taking Root' wrap-around services program. Textbook public-health framing of violent crime / illegal drugs (rubric-adverse on q3 — strongest in the batch). HARD FALSE sanctity_of_life q1: NYT op-ed and X post May 2022 — 'No matter what the law in Virginia says, I will not prosecute a woman for having an abortion, or for being suspected of inducing one' (wtop.com + x.com/stevedescano/1531594579027415045 + foxnews.com). Sanctuary-style prosecutorial declaration — the cleanest abortion-sanctuary hit on local q1 in the VA batch. HARD FALSE border_immigration q2: written office policy in force for 6 years until May 2026 directing prosecutors to 'make charging and plea decisions that limit or avoid immigration consequences' — DOJ launched civil rights investigation May 7 2026 into 'preferential treatment' for illegal immigrants on this exact policy; policy was removed from the office website before Capitol Hill testimony (justice.gov + washingtonexaminer.com + wjla.com Apr 2026 House Judiciary). Multi-vector affirmative sanctuary-style policy — strongest border-cell FALSE in the batch. NOT scored sanctity_of_life q4: major identifiable money is Soros JPSP + New Virginia Majority PAC, not the specifically-named PP/NARAL/EMILY's List PACs — conservative null absent a direct PP/NARAL/EMILY donor record. NOT scored refuse_state_overreach q0-q4: rubric direction is COMPLEX — Descano refuses to enforce state laws he disagrees with (abortion restrictions, marijuana, drug-mandatory minimums) but in the rubric-ADVERSE direction; the cell text reads as defending citizens from ungodly overreach, which doesn't quite map to a prosecutor refusing in the opposite direction. Null per discipline rather than overreach the rubric. NOT scored biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / christian_liberty / economic_stewardship / election_integrity / self_defense — no DA-office actions located despite extensive Wikipedia/press/campaign-site review. 0 TRUE / 6 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — coherent prototype Soros-DA progressive prosecutor with abortion-sanctuary declaration + 6-yr immigration-leniency policy + textbook decarceral framing. Active DOJ civil rights probe makes this one of the most well-documented LOCAL records in the entire VA batch. Recommend revisiting after the May 2026 DOJ probe outcome and any 2026-27 cycle.

1.note: NYT op-ed and X post May 2022 — 'No matter what the law in Virginia says, I will not prosecute a woman for having an abortion, or for being suspected of inducing one.' Sanctuary-style prosecutorial declaration on the rubric-adverse direction of q1 ('would prosecute violations of state pro-life law' inverse).

0.note: Tweeted 'There is no greater institutional obstacle to real reform than police unions. If you spend your career working for that obstacle, you don't believe in reform' — affirmative anti-LE-institution advocacy clearing the HIGH BAR on q0 in the rubric-adverse direction.

1.note: 'From his first day in office, Steve has not asked for cash bail. After formally ending cash bail in 2020...'; declines marijuana possession prosecution; 'I'm against the death penalty, period, full stop'; named 'America's Worst Prosecutor' by AEI; active DOJ civil rights investigation opened May 7 2026. Multi-vector decarceral/Soros-DA policy posture (rubric-adverse on q1).

2.note: Office policy avoids overcharging/mandatory minimums, refuses 3rd-strike enhancements for petty larceny, prioritizes diversion over conviction; multiple high-profile dismissals/reductions in violent cases (Richard Kenneth Cox, Marvin Morales-Ortez, Jalloh probation release) prompted recall petitions and the DOJ probe. Failure-to-punish posture (rubric-adverse on q2 proportional-punishment cell).

3.note: Direct quote on addiction — 'addiction is an illness, not a crime'; uses root-causes/diversion framing throughout; created Diversion Coordinator and 'Taking Root' wrap-around services program. Textbook public-health framing of crime in his own voice (rubric-adverse on q3, strongest in batch).

2.note: Written office policy in force 6 years until removed May 2026: 'Wherever possible, Steve will make charging and plea decisions that limit or avoid immigration consequences.' DOJ launched civil rights investigation May 7 2026 into 'preferential treatment' for illegal immigrants on this exact policy; policy was removed from the office website before Capitol Hill testimony. Affirmative sanctuary-style prosecutorial policy (rubric-adverse on q2).

profile.religion: Christian — she and her husband co-teach a high school Sunday school class at their unnamed church (campaign-site bio). Denomination not publicly disclosed.
profile.education: B.A. Psychology, cum laude, Amherst College; MSW (Direct Practice with Children, Youth, and Families), Washington University in St. Louis; MPP (Education/Social/Family Policy), Georgetown University (CROCUS fellow on parental involvement in juvenile justice). Registered Dance/Movement Therapist (R-DMT).
profile.background: Virginia public-school graduate; originally from Fauquier County, relocated to Spotsylvania. Married, mother of four. Worked in Spotsylvania County Public Schools (SCPS) 2016–May 2023 as a creative-arts/dance-movement therapist serving special-education students; currently a school social worker for Fredericksburg City Public Schools. Active in local dance and theater. First elected to Spotsylvania School Board, Chancellor District, Nov 7 2023, defeating Jordan Lynch 52.37%–46.45%; sworn Jan 2024; current term ends Dec 31 2027 — next election Nov 2027. Replaced Dawn Shelley (a Mark Taylor critic who didn't seek re-election). Elected Vice Chair Jan 2025 (5-2 vote). Campaign brand was 'restore decency to the school board,' with secondary priorities on student mental health, teacher retention, and special education.

belen-rodas.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania School Board Chancellor District, sworn Jan 2024, term ends 12/31/2027 — next election Nov 2027. PARTY: de jure nonpartisan but de facto Democratic-aligned reform faction — refused formal Spotsy Democratic Committee endorsement BUT accepted PAC money from Democratic-aligned PAC + endorsed by the Spotsylvania Education Association (teachers' union); no GOP / Moms for Liberty / Patriot Mothers support. Ran explicitly against the Taylor/Twigg conservative majority. Update party Unknown→D. RELIGION: Christian, Sunday-school teacher with husband (denomination undisclosed). EDUCATION: Amherst BA Psychology (cum laude) + Wash U MSW + Georgetown MPP + R-DMT. ELECTED VICE CHAIR Jan 2025 (5-2 vote). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: voted with the 5-0 reform majority on the late-2024 revised library-materials/instructional-material challenge policy (fredericksburg.com 12/2024) that softened the prior Taylor-era removal regime — returned book-appropriateness decisions to the superintendent and principals, the OPPOSITE direction of the rubric's 'voted to REMOVE CRT/SOGI/comp-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials.' Named as defendant in the Jennifer Peterson petition seeking removal of sexually-explicit books from libraries — fxbgadvance.com (4/2026 'Book Banners Are Back') confirms the lawsuit posture against her board's permissive direction. Multi-vector rubric-adverse posture on q2. HARD FALSE public_justice q3: school social worker by profession; campaign #2 priority was 'student mental health'; publicly pleaded with the General Assembly Nov 2025 for more mental-health funding to address 'extreme' student crises (fredericksburgfreepress.com 11/17/2025) — framing youth challenges through the clinical/mental-health public-health lens, the rubric-adverse direction on q3 (parallel to Robertson/Jordan/Doyle FALSEs in prior Richmond + Norfolk batches). NOT scored family_child_sovereignty q1 (parental notification on trans/medical): the Youngkin model trans policy was adopted by Spotsy SB Aug 14 2023 BEFORE Rodas was seated (Jan 2024); her faction has not been documented rescinding it as of available reporting — null per discipline absent a Rodas-attributed vote. NOT scored biblical_marriage q2: no recorded Rodas vote on transgender bathroom/sports/pronoun policy (Youngkin model predates her seating); no public statement either rejecting or affirming trans ideology — null per discipline. NOT scored biblical_marriage q4: no documented Rodas vote on Pride displays / Day of Silence / LGBTQ promotion specifically; her library-policy posture is permissive but no specific LGBTQ-content vote located. NOT scored christian_liberty q0/q1/q2/q3: no documented positions on school prayer, conscience exemptions, pronoun mandates, or SB invocations. NOT scored sanctity_of_life / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / self_defense / election_integrity / economic_stewardship — no quote-bearing positions located. RICH CONTEXTUAL MARKERS: voted with 5-0 reform majority to FIRE Superintendent Mark Taylor March 12 2024 (fredericksburgfreepress.com); voted with 5-0 majority on 2024 revised library-materials policy (fredericksburg.com 12/2024); publicly characterized prior conservative majority as 'completely extreme and chaotic and irrational' (campaign-trail quote); named defendant in BOTH the Mark Taylor wrongful-termination lawsuit AND the Jennifer Peterson book-removal petition. 0 TRUE / 2 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — coherent reform-faction D-aligned SB member whose distinctive features are the post-Taylor library-policy reversal + mental-health-crisis framing. Personal Christian faith is genuine and disclosed (Sunday-school teaching) but does not surface scoreable culture-war positions absent direct votes/quotes. Recommend revisiting after any 2026-27 culture-issue SB votes or if her faction moves to rescind the Youngkin model trans policy.

2.note: Voted with the 5-0 reform majority on the late-2024 revised library-materials / instructional-material challenge policy that softened the prior Taylor-era removal regime — returned book-appropriateness decisions to the superintendent and principals, the OPPOSITE direction of the rubric's 'voted to REMOVE CRT/SOGI/comp-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials.' Named as defendant in the Jennifer Peterson book-removal petition (fxbgadvance.com 'Book Banners Are Back' April 2026).

3.note: School social worker by profession; campaign #2 priority was 'student mental health'; publicly pleaded with the General Assembly Nov 2025 for more mental-health funding to address 'extreme' student crises — framing youth challenges through the clinical/mental-health public-health lens (rubric-adverse on q3, parallel to Robertson/Jordan/Doyle FALSEs).

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no statement of faith located in her campaign site, Ballotpedia, VPAP, or press coverage reviewed.
profile.education: M.A. Instructional Technology, George Mason University; Ph.D. Instructional Leadership, Virginia Commonwealth University. (The 'Dr.' prefix is an academic Ph.D., not medical or Ed.D.)
profile.background: Entering her 28th year in education at the time of the 2023 campaign — career K-12 educator across high school, middle school, and elementary in a Northern Virginia school division; taught math and special education, served as a math specialist, currently works as an instructional math coach. Previously ran unsuccessfully for school board in Stafford County before relocating to Spotsylvania. Mother, public-school graduate, public-school advocate. Resident of Courtland District, Spotsylvania. First elected to Spotsylvania School Board, Courtland District, Nov 2023; sworn Jan 2024; current term ends Dec 31 2027 — next election Nov 2027. Replaced Rabih Abuismail (a key Twigg-era conservative-majority bloc member).

carol-medawar.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania School Board Courtland District, sworn Jan 2024, term ends 12/31/2027 — next election Nov 2027. PARTY: de jure nonpartisan but de facto Democratic-aligned reform faction — ran on the 'Energized for Change' PAC slate (Eileen Filler-Corn's Democratic PAC) with Rodas and Daniels; received funding from the 7th Congressional District Democratic Party. Replaced Twigg-bloc Rabih Abuismail. Update party Unknown→D. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. EDUCATION: GMU M.A. Instructional Technology + VCU Ph.D. Instructional Leadership (academic, not medical/Ed.D.). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q2: voted with the 5-member reform majority on the 2024 book/library policy that returned book-appropriateness decisions to the superintendent and principals — clear loosening of the prior Taylor-era removal regime (fxbgadvance.com 'Book Banners Are Back' + fredericksburg.com 12/2024 instructional-material challenge policy). HARD FALSE family_child_sovereignty q3: voted with the same 5-member reform majority on the late-2024 instructional-material challenge policy that tightened the challenge process (limiting parent-driven challenges of 'sexually explicit' content) — the opposite direction from the conservative book-removal stance and from the rubric's age-verification / sexualized-minor-content cell. HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q2: endorsed and funded by Democratic 'Energized for Change' PAC and 7th CD Democrats running explicitly against the Twigg/Abuismail Youngkin-trans-policy majority; replaced Abuismail (the loudest pro-policy vote on the Aug 2023 Youngkin model adoption); ACLU-VA tracked her race as a reform pickup over the LGBTQ-restrictive incumbent bloc (acluva.org press release on the Youngkin policy + fxbgadvance.com fundraising coverage). HARD FALSE biblical_marriage q4: same slate/funding pattern; ACLU-VA tracked her race as a reform pickup over the bloc that adopted the Youngkin trans-restrictive guidance (aclu.org Virginia school board elections Spotsylvania page). HARD FALSE economic_stewardship q2: voted FOR the FY25 $480M SCPS budget (6-1) with a 5% teacher raise + step + 6% COLA; publicly defended central-office salaries against supervisor cuts Feb 2026 and framed cuts as harmful — explicit pro-spending posture, NOT budget-discipline (fxbgadvance.com 'spotsylvania-school-board-approves' + fredericksburgfreepress.com 2/10/2026 central-office-salaries article). NOT scored family_child_sovereignty q1 (parental notification on trans/medical): the Aug 14 2023 Youngkin trans-policy adoption vote (4-2 in favor) predates Medawar's January 2024 swearing-in; no subsequent recorded Medawar vote rescinding or affirming it. NOT scored public_justice q0 (SROs): SCPS continues SRO program via Sheriff MOU; no Medawar-specific affirmative pro-SRO statement found — null per HIGH BAR discipline (budget-line votes insufficient). NOT scored christian_liberty / sanctity_of_life / refuse_state_overreach / border_immigration / election_integrity / self_defense — no documented positions in scope. Voted with the 5-0 reform majority to FIRE Superintendent Mark Taylor March 12 2024 (fredericksburgfreepress.com). 0 TRUE / 5 FALSE on cited cells = 0% — most rubric-adverse cited record in the Spotsy SB batch; reform-faction D-aligned member with explicit anti-book-removal + anti-Youngkin-trans-policy slate posture + pro-spending budget posture. Recommend revisiting after any 2026-27 cycle.

2.note: Voted with the 5-member reform majority on the 2024 book/library policy that returned book-appropriateness decisions to the superintendent and principals AND on the late-2024 instructional-material challenge policy that tightened the challenge process (limiting parent-driven challenges of 'sexually explicit' content) — clear loosening of the prior Taylor-era removal regime (rubric-adverse on q2 'voted to REMOVE CRT/SOGI/comp-sex-ed/gender-ideology materials' inverse).

Endorsed and funded by Democratic 'Energized for Change' PAC and 7th CD Democrats running explicitly against the Twigg/Abuismail Youngkin-trans-policy majority; replaced Abuismail (the loudest pro-policy vote on the Aug 2023 Youngkin model adoption). ACLU-VA press release on the Youngkin policy. No public statement supporting the Youngkin model policy located despite extensive review.

4.note: Same Energized-for-Change-PAC + 7th CD Democratic slate pattern. ACLU-VA tracked her race as a reform pickup over the LGBTQ-restrictive incumbent bloc that adopted the Youngkin trans-restrictive guidance.

2.note: Voted FOR the FY25 $480M SCPS budget (6-1) with a 5% teacher raise + step + 6% COLA; publicly defended central-office salaries against supervisor cuts Feb 2026 and framed cuts as harmful — explicit pro-spending posture, NOT budget-discipline (rubric-adverse on q2).

set.office: County Board of Supervisors — Battlefield District

profile.religion: NOT PUBLICLY DISCLOSED — no statement of faith located in his official county bio, campaign site, or press coverage reviewed.
profile.education: Germanna Community College; political science coursework at Virginia Commonwealth University (no degree completion confirmed on official county page).
profile.background: Small business owner — runs a home remodeling company started by his father. Married to Melissa; two daughters in Spotsylvania County Public Schools. Lives in the Breezewood section of Spotsylvania ~30 years. First elected to the Spotsylvania County Board of Supervisors, Battlefield District, Nov 2003; long-tenured incumbent serving ~22 years across multiple re-elections. Re-elected Nov 2025 by 16 votes over Baron Braswell (rematch); confirmed by recount Dec 2025; term ends Dec 31 2029 — next election Nov 2029. Unanimously elected 2025 Board Chair by the BoS at the Jan 2025 meeting. Unsuccessfully ran for VA state Senate in 2007. Politically: maverick/establishment-moderate Republican repeatedly at odds with the Spotsy GOP committee — the Republican State Central Committee had to overrule the local Spotsy GOP in 2025 to give him the nomination after he criticized them publicly ('I'm a Republican, and these continued attempts to sabotage a Republican candidacy are sad and pathetic').

chris-yakabouski.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Spotsylvania County BoS Battlefield District, ~22-year incumbent (since 2003), re-elected Nov 2025 by 16 votes (recount-confirmed Dec 2025); current term ends 12/31/2029 — next election Nov 2029. 2025 Board Chair (unanimously elected by BoS at Jan 2025 meeting). PARTY: Republican CONFIRMED (Spotsylvania County 2025 candidates list 'Republican party nominated'); the Republican State Central Committee overruled the local Spotsy GOP committee in 2025 to give him the nomination after he criticized them publicly. Update party Unknown→R. RELIGION: not publicly disclosed. EDUCATION: Germanna CC + VCU political science coursework (no degree completion confirmed). BACKGROUND: small-biz home remodeling company started by his father; lives in Breezewood section ~30 years. HARD TRUE self_defense q0: Spotsylvania County BoS adopted the 2A Sanctuary resolution Dec 10 2019 (passed unanimously per multiple reports; Yakabouski was sitting Battlefield Supervisor at the time; Spotsy listed on the va2nd.com 2A Sanctuary tracker; reaffirmed Jan 30 2026 'message to the state: we are still a 2A Sanctuary county' per fredericksburgfreepress.com). 'Constitutional carry without permit requirements' cell — supported via the broader 2A sanctuary refusal posture combined with the CCW-fee-reduction vote. HARD TRUE self_defense q1: publicly proposed reducing Spotsy CCW permit fee, self-identified as 'a strong believer in the Second Amendment,' quoted: 'If this is a little thing we can do to make it easier for folks to exercise their Second Amendment right, then I will gladly move it forward' (fredericksburg.com 'spotsylvania-official-wants-to-reduce-fee-for-concealed-gun-permit'). Affirmative gun-friendly local-policy posture on the rubric-favorable direction. HARD TRUE refuse_state_overreach q4: the Spotsy 2A Sanctuary resolution adopted Dec 10 2019 during the McAuliffe/Northam VA-blue-trifecta gun-control push was a vote to refuse local enforcement of state-level gun-control mandates the BoS viewed as unconstitutional — direct hit on 'refused to enforce unconstitutional state directives.'
